titleStmt.title: SII 38.293: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 293. (A.R. No. 293 of 1912.) KUṞAṄGAṆIMUṬṬAM, CHEYYAR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT. On the west wall, maṇḍapa, infront of the central shrine, Koyyāmalarīśvara Temple.

summary.p: This inscription registers a gift of 18(1/8) narpaḻaṅkāśu by the ūravar of Pallavapuram in Ērikīḻ-nāḍu in Kāliyūr-kōṭṭam in order to burn a perpetual lamp to the god of Tirukkuraṅkaṇimuṭṭam. This amount was divided among the four persons. 1. Vāḷavaṉ received 5 kāśu. 2. Viḷandai-uḍaiyāṉ (5(1/8) kāśu.) 3. Chōḻiyavaraiyan 4 kāśu. 4. Nochchilkiḻāṉ 4 kāśu.
It is also recorded that these four persons undertook the responsibility of measuring out 9 nāḻi, 1 āḻākku and 1 piḍi of ghee by the measure muḻarivaṉ every month to the temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . kulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yā
ṇṭu Oṉpatāvatu kāliyūrkoṭṭattu Eriki
ḻnāṭṭu pallavapurattu Ūravarom tirukku
raṅkaṇimuṭṭamuṭaiyāṟku cetarāyar tirununtāviḷakkukku viṭṭa naṟpaḻaṅkācu patineṭṭe kāleyaraikkālil Ivvūril vāḷuvan
koṇṭa kācu Aiñcum viḷantayuṭaiyān koṇṭa raik kālumMay be restored as kācu aiñceyaraikkālum because after adding all the gift of kāsus the balance should be aiñceyaraikkāl coḻiyavarayan koṇṭa kācu nālum noccil
kiḻānkoṇṭa kācu nālum Inālu kuṭiyum Upaiya māsam Oṉṟukku muḻurivaṉāl Oṉpatu nāḻi Āḻākke Orupiṭi tiru viḷakku
Eṇṇai koyilile tiṅkaḷ mutalile ĀḷakkaRead aḷakkakkaṭavom Ivai EṇIncomplete. There is another inscription engraved in the 17th century characters by the side of this record and it runs as follows 1 pelipiṭam 2 uṭaiyavarum 3 tirupulivaṉam 4 tevaṇapaṭṭar 5 tampāraḻakaṉ nayiṉār 6 upaiyam .
Digital edition of SII 38.293 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
